item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Grand. Anyway, allow me to modify the above recommendation to:

An illustration depicting 'Ascetic Sun', a figure held to be the previous incarnation of the Victorious Fighting Buddha, a deity revered by the 'Ascetics to Heaven's Pillar'. Other sacred sites associated with 'Ascetic Sun' include Mount Tongbai in Henan Province and a rock formation in the Himalayas.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

So, coming up with a crosslink for this article left me stumped until I PMed MrWrong who was kind enough to share in the lore behind this article and provide some insight into possible crosslinks that would work. According to MrWrong:

The Ascetics are literally themed after Journey to the West, a famous Chinese novel, and Ascetic Sun is literally Sun Wukong. And the site has at least two articles that feature or allude to Sun Wukong, like SCP-2711 and SCP-2178.

As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

An illustration depicting 'Ascetic Sun'. According to the 'Ascetics to Heaven's Pillar', 'Ascetic Sun' is held to be the previous incarnation of the Victorious Fighting Buddha, a deity revered by the group.

To this:

An illustration depicting 'Ascetic Sun'. According to the 'Ascetics to Heaven's Pillar', 'Ascetic Sun' is held to be the previous incarnation of the Victorious Fighting Buddha, a deity revered by the group. See SCP documents SCP-2711 and SCP-2178 for more details.

Thank you MrWrong.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I was to nitpick anything, I would argue that the blackboxing of the site number doesn't really add any flavor and comes across more along the lines of blackboxing for the sake of blackboxing. Same with the recovery month and day.

Aye. The blackboxed site was more of my tribute to the original version, which blackboxed the site as well.

As for the recovery date and month, it's all me and my personal quirk for SCP articles. Always include recovery, show the year and location, blackbox the month and day.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, I'll be honest, this is the first version of this skip that I have read. That disclaimer out of the way, lets get in.

This article is alright. Not amazing, or terrible, but alright. For the first paragraph or so, I was really kind of trudging along, as it seemed to be typical "parasite squick." However, the tie in with the Buddhism adds another dimension to this that really saves it from a downvote. I can totally buy into a group using these bugs in their religious practice.

If I was to nitpick anything, I would argue that the blackboxing of the site number doesn't really add any flavor and comes across more along the lines of blackboxing for the sake of blackboxing. Same with the recovery month and day.

In the end. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of my greatest regrets is that I'll never be able to read/speak all the languages of Earth… I do have a huge book with description, analysis and 'dictionary' of common words for about 3000 of them, though!

I think very few people in the world would be exposed to every kind of language there is, but spaces like the Internet are perfect places to engage in other types of speech and writing.

P.S. PM-ed you something to sate your curiosity about my "multi-lingual"1 capacity.

Footnotes
1. Yeah, right… — says me with sarcasm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oops, sorry, Mr Wrong, I misplaced my bookmark for this and forgot about it!

Chinese characters: fascinating.
One of my greatest regrets is that I'll never be able to read/speak all the languages of Earth… I do have a huge book with description, analysis and 'dictionary' of common words for about 3000 of them, though!

Arterial language: no, I didn't Google it - thought it was something you made up! I've never seen/heard that expression before.

I personally regard my first language, native language and mother tongue as separate languages altogether.

What do you mean? There are three different languages? Just curious!

Sokushinbutsu: ah, OK, got it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nicely rewritten. The addition of Buddhists gives it that extra level of creepiness that the original lacked. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interestingly, in Chinese characters, the words for worm (蠕虫), cicada (蝉) and wasp (黄蜂) all have the 虫 radical (which is usually used for creeping animals and derived from logo-graphic models of a snake).

I find it facinating when you see these "inherent human perception" things in language- one of the generic words for creeping/unpleasant creatures in English, "vermin", is derived from the latin word for worm, "vermis"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I have been figuring out how to amend that line for a while. Thanks for giving me this tip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While inside human ears for at least a period of one week, SCP-1186 will emit sounds at an undetermined audio frequency.

You might swap this out for

After occupying the human ear for a period of one week, SCP-1186 will begin emitting vibrations directly to the skull, which are percieved as sound in a manner similar to certain forms of hearing aid.

"Undetermined audio frequency" sounds like no one's bothered to look at an equalizer graph, whereas Bone Conduction is a fairly common and simple way of putting sound directly into one's head even when hearing is impaired. (They also have microphones that can record this way too, so it doesn't prevent them from recording the chants.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, it went away and came back again! I always liked this article, it's always been memorable, and I think you've only improved it with the addition of creepy Buddhists. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Except it's a cicada, not a worm. And that personally, the picture reminds me more of wasps…! ;>

Interestingly, in Chinese characters, the words for worm (蠕虫), cicada (蝉) and wasp (黄蜂) all have the 虫 radical (which is usually used for creeping animals and derived from logo-graphic models of a snake).

Why the microphones, I wonder? Does the Foundation want to hear every single chant the things emit?

As a convenient way of hearing things. I will imagine that they are saying chants that have yet existed or will never exist.

What do you mean by 'arterial language'? Just curious!

Have you Googled it? It is essentially first language. I settled for this since I find "first", "native" and "mother" a bit colloquial for my tastes, and I personally regard my first language, native language and mother tongue as separate languages altogether.

This reminds me of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sokushinbutsu
Is this what you're alluding to? Wouldn't the Foundation know about this method of preservation and investigate?

Well, the sokushinbutsu practice self-mummification. This would be more of a perfectly preserved human body without any signs of any mummification techniques used on it. It is kept with the Foundation since the preservation method is unknown to the current understanding of science.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A new understanding of the word earworm. Except it's a cicada, not a worm. And that personally, the picture reminds me more of wasps…! ;>

I corrected 'are to taken' into 'are to be taken' (2nd paragraph of containment procedures).

Small nitpicks:

+ show block
– hide block

Each unit is to be constructed to resemble the human auditory system, and equipped with high-power microphones.

Why the microphones, I wonder? Does the Foundation want to hear every single chant the things emit?

At least 140 additional units are to be made available as reserve habitats for SCP-1186 instances. The maximum number of SCP-1186 instances permitted in Foundation captivity is 140;

If the maximum is 140, why 'at least' 140 additional units?

Due to the loss of endolymph, hosts of SCP-1186 regularly experience deafness and balance disorder.

You repeat this information again two paragraphs later (see below), which is a bit jarring. Since you give a bit more details there, perhaps delete it here?

in respective hosts' arterial language

What do you mean by 'arterial language'? Just curious!

These sounds are inaudible in environments with sound levels of at least 50 dB, but hosts are known to suffer from afflictions such as headaches and nausea in such environments. In 2% of known cases, non-lethal damage to hosts' lung and nervous tissues is present. Conversely, in environments with sound levels below 50 dB, the severity of the aforementioned afflictions tend to subside and audibility of SCP-1186's sounds increases. Regardless of the environment, loss of hearing and balance will occur among hosts of SCP-1186.

This paragraph is a bit confusing, IMO.
1) The second sentence (lung & nervous tissues) feels out of place. Perhaps move it elsewhere? And BTW, why/how damage to lung and nervous tissues from a sound and consumption of endolymph?
2) the description of what happens below or above 50dB is a a bit convoluted and not easy to follow. Could you perhaps reword for clarity?

Despite its age, no signs of decomposition are found; mechanism of preservation is unknown.

This reminds me of https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sokushinbutsu
Is this what you're alluding to? Wouldn't the Foundation know about this method of preservation and investigate?

All this is not enough to prevent me from upvoting!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this a lot when I reviewed it, and I like it a lot now. Very well written and just weird. +1

item.title: Rewrite Acknowledge, Thanks, Attribution, and Other Matters
item.content:encoded:
This article is a rewrite of the previous SCP-1186 by Bunton. Special thanks to Bunton for allowing me permission to go through this rewrite attempt and to djkaktus (and the Rewrite Team) for being my rewrite contact.

Also thanks to people like AJMansfield, Dr Solo, Quikngrvn (for getting me the direction for measurements and quantification) and many others along the way.

Images are attributed accordingly:

  • Image 1 by Graham Wise of Wikimedia Commons (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Cicada_nymph_(14338617637).jpg) under Creative Commons Attribution 2
  • Image 2 (https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Xyj-sun_wukong.jpg) under Public Domain

Due to the Grand Crosslinking, this article now features:

  • SCP-2711 (定海神针 如意金箍棒) by SunnyClockwork
  • SCP-2178 (Great Sage) by Chubert

And so what's going on?

+ show block
– hide block

The corpse found at the end is actually Xuanzang, the monk in Journey to the West. The Ascetics' "gods" are the disciples from the novel, but Xuanzang is not worshipped since the cult holds that he is back on Earth to help worshippers.

And Tianzhu is an old term for India (specifically named after its status as Buddhism's origin).

Material on Bunton's SCP-1186

+ View SCP File
- Hide SCP File

Item #: SCP-1186

Object Class: Safe

Special Containment Procedures: Multiple SCP-1186 nymphs are contained in Sites-██, ███ and ███. An overall maximum of one-hundred (100) specimens are to be contained, with a minimum of 15 (fifteen) per assigned Site. All specimens must be held within a completely sound-proofed container.

Excess specimens are to be removed and incinerated while nymphs. Under no circumstances should imago specimens be removed less than 3 (three) days following death.

Feeding is to be carried out as follows: the endolymph of cattle (kept on-site specifically for this purpose) is to be extracted weekly, and then inserted into basic, reusable models of the human auditory system (referred to as the █████ model: please see Document 1186-1). At this time, it is decided that reproduction should be allowed to occur as normal; however, select specimens are to be removed and incinerated (through the use of sound-proofed biohazard suits) should the population increase past acceptable limits. SCP-1186 imago are to be handled separately from nymphs, and should be dealt with in such a way as to facilitate breeding: please refer to Document 1186-2 for further information on this.

Any SCP-1186 uncontained specimens should be disposed of at the earliest possible opportunity, if no special circumstances apply. In this event, wild SCP-1186 eggs should be incinerated and the areas housing them fumigated, with appropriate cover stories provided to local media.

Description: SCP-1186 (Tibicen auritus) is a species of the family Cicadidae. SCP-1186 imagos display no active anomalous behaviour, but lack sexual organs and a reproductive capacity. 4-5 (four to five) hours after the death of a SCP-1186 imago, 2-3 (two to three) SCP-1186 larvae will spontaneously appear in its vicinity, provided the imago's body remains intact. This process has not yet been observed or recorded.

Unlike most members of the order Hemiptera, SCP-1186 nymphs are not herbivorous - instead of consuming arboreal sap, nymphs are known to drain the endolymph from human cochlear ducts. Other adaptations, such as a specially elongated and retractable proboscis and an internal structure with the function of efficiently digesting substances with a high potassium concentration are also notable, though not independently unusual. Most specimens of SCP-1186 are able to fulfill their dietary requirements by feeding once every 1-2 (one to two) weeks.

Specimens accomplish feeding through the use of a combination of undetermined audio frequencies (all of which are estimated to be extremely close to the upper range of human hearing) that cause adverse psychological and physical effects. While the sounds produced are imperceptible and asymptomatic in noisy environments (>50 dB), they cause a phenomenon usually described as "deafening silence" by those who experience it in quiet places (<10 dB). Attempts at replicating the effects of this sound through other means have invariably been unsuccessful.

The first effect of this sound is a headache and nausea, followed by extreme dizziness and unconsciousness. Extremely close exposure has been shown to cause damage to lung and neural tissue; however, fatal injury rarely occurs.

SCP-1186 will find and incapacitate prey in quiet environment, then crawl behind the target's ear following the onset of unconsciousness. Specimens will then use their proboscis to puncture the skin and drain the inner ear, a process which will often leave victims permanently deaf in addition to severely impairing balance.

Discussion page 1

Discussion page 2
